content.text: official client SDKs for seven programming languages
(shell command-line tool). All official SDKs handle authentication, request formatting, error handling, retries, and streaming automatically. If you prefer not to use an SDK, you can call the
, Postman, or any HTTP client library, sending JSON requests to
header for authentication. The raw HTTP API uses REST architecture with JSON request/response bodies, and all requests require authentication via API key.
(language-specific patterns, e.g., builders for Java, async/await for TypeScript)
(complete type definitions, though some languages like Go use structs instead of compile-time types)
(automatic API key handling from environment variables)
(2 retries by default for connection errors, 408, 409, 429, ≥500)
(specific exception classes mapped to HTTP status codes)

ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
defer cancel()

message, err := client.Messages.Create(ctx, params)

require 'anthropic'

client = Anthropic::Client.new(api_key: ENV['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'])

message = client.messages.create(
  model: 'claude-opus-4-8',
  max_tokens: 1024,
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello, Claude' }]
)

puts message.content[0].text

stream = client.messages.create_stream(
  model: 'claude-opus-4-8',
  max_tokens: 1024,
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello, Claude' }]
)

stream.each do |event|
  puts event.type
end

# First-time setup: authenticate with your account
claude

# Then use directly in shell
claude "What is the Zen of Python?"

# Use in shell scripts
response=$(claude "Explain async/await in JavaScript")
echo $response

# Connect to MCP servers
claude --mcp-server mcp-server-name
Works in any language (even without a native SDK)
Useful for debugging, testing, or shell scripting

curl https://api.anthropic.com/v1/complete \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -d '{
    "model": "claude-2",
    "prompt": "Human: Hello\\nAssistant:",
    "max_tokens_to_sample": 200
  }'

curl https://api.anthropic.com/v1/complete \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "x-api-key: $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" \
  -d '{
    "model": "claude-2",
    "prompt": "Human: Write a friendly 50-word introduction\\nAssistant:",
    "max_tokens_to_sample": 200
  }'

curl https://api.anthropic.com/v1/embeddings \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -d '{
    "model": "embed-1",
    "input": "Text to convert to an embedding"
  }'

import anthropic
from anthropic import AnthropicBedrockMantle

client = AnthropicBedrockMantle()  # Uses AWS IAM

import { AnthropicBedrockMantle } from "@anthropic-ai/bedrock-sdk";

const client = new AnthropicBedrockMantle();

from anthropic import AnthropicVertex

client = AnthropicVertex()

import { AnthropicVertex } from "@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk";

const client = new AnthropicVertex();

from anthropic import AnthropicFoundry

client = new AnthropFoundry();

import { AnthropicFoundry } from "@anthropic-ai/foundry-sdk";

const client = new AnthropicFoundry();

import { AnthropicAws } from "@anthropic-ai/aws-sdk";

const client = new AnthropicAws({ workspaceId: "your-workspace-id" });
Choosing Between SDK vs Raw HTTP
Use an official SDK for production apps, raw HTTP for quick prototypes, debugging, or shell scripts.

Use an official SDK for production apps (better error handling, type safety, built-in features), and raw HTTP for quick prototypes, debugging, or shell scripts where SDK setup is impractical.
